pattern: Reduce each customer to a bit in a small mask, then use a submask dynamic program to decide, value stock by value stock, which still-unsatisfied group of customers that stock alone could complete.
hints: There are at most fifty distinct values and at most ten customers, so represent the set of already-satisfied customers as bits in a mask rather than reasoning about the raw quantity list directly.

For a table of masks known to be fully satisfiable, feed in one distinct value's total stock at a time and see which additional group of not-yet-satisfied customers that single stock could complete on its own.

For each satisfiable mask, enumerate submasks of its complement whose combined demand fits the current stock, and mark the union as newly satisfiable; stop as soon as the mask covering every customer is reached.

edge_inputs.description: one customer exactly matches the stock of a repeated value
three customers each take one of three distinct values
fifty distinct values, each appearing once, one small customer
single customer consumes the entire stock of one value
total elements suffice but no single value has enough copies
two customers compete for a scarce value that cannot cover both
quantities match totals exactly only when paired with the right values

stress.description: large nums array over few distinct values against up to ten random customer orders
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(100000, size))
    unique_vals = rng.randint(1, 50)
    vals = list(range(1, unique_vals + 1))
    nums = [rng.choice(vals) for _ in range(n)]
    m = rng.randint(1, 10)
    quantity = [rng.randint(1, max(1, min(100000, n // m))) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums, quantity]
stress.note: quantities are scaled down from n so both feasible and infeasible cases occur
